candidate_problems.text: Are the five proposed load-bearing conditions jointly necessary, or can distinct structural substitutes generate equally stable cooperation without preserving the same coupling? The conjecture claims non-interchangeability, but it remains open whether there are multiple realizable architectures of cooperation with different load-bearing details, or whether this specific bundle marks a genuine universality class.

What are the threshold and phase-transition conditions under which the feedback loop among reputation, partner choice, observability, future stakes, and norm legibility becomes self-sustaining rather than collapsing into noise, exclusion, or opportunism? The conjecture is structurally compelling, but it leaves unresolved the quantitative boundaries, nonlinearities, and path dependencies that determine when cooperation scales or fails.

How robust is the mechanism under strategic manipulation of the informational layer—false signaling, reputation gaming, collusion, norm entrepreneurship, or institutional capture? The conjecture treats visible and interpretable histories as cooperation-supporting, but an open question is when those same channels become attack surfaces that stabilize domination, cartelization, or factional punishment instead of genuine cooperation.
